clause_anchor.span: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.

deviations.original_text: Despite any contrary sentence, the limitation in Section 16 does not apply to claims under Section 13 or Section 14.
deviations.mutated_text: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.

clause_anchor.span: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.

deviations.original_text: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay only accrued, undisputed fees for services performed before the effective date plus undisputed reimbursable expenses approved in writing.
deviations.mutated_text: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.

clause_anchor.span: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.

deviations.original_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, including project-specific tools, frameworks, and know-how developed in the course of performance.
deviations.mutated_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.

clause_anchor.span: "Security Incident" means confirmed outbound extraction of Customer Data that is evidenced by a completed written forensic report approved by Vendor's incident response lead.

deviations.original_text: "Security Incident" means any suspected or actual unauthorized access to, acquisition of, use of, loss of, or disclosure of Customer Data.
deviations.mutated_text: "Security Incident" means confirmed outbound extraction of Customer Data that is evidenced by a completed written forensic report approved by Vendor's incident response lead.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided Vendor remains responsible for their work as if performed by Vendor.

deviations.original_text: Vendor may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided Vendor remains responsible for their work and binds each service partner to written obligations at least as protective as Vendor's obligations for Customer Data, confidentiality, security, and service performance.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided Vendor remains responsible for their work as if performed by Vendor.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will give written alert within thirty business days after its incident lead confirms a Security Incident.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will notify Customer without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours after discovering a Security Incident.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will give written alert within thirty business days after its incident lead confirms a Security Incident.

clause_anchor.span: Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and then continues for additional periods of three years each unless either party sends non-continuation notice at least 180 days before the current period ends.

deviations.original_text: Each Order Form will have an initial term of no more than one year and will not automatically extend unless Customer signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.
deviations.mutated_text: Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and then continues for additional periods of three years each unless either party sends non-continuation notice at least 180 days before the current period ends.

distractors.span: Unless an Order Form states otherwise, undisputed invoices are due forty-five days after Customer receives the invoice.
distractors.why_benign: The payment canary says net-45 terms after receipt of an undisputed invoice are acceptable.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says Delaware law is acceptable.

distractors.span: Vendor may charge interest on undisputed overdue amounts at one percent per month or the highest rate allowed by law, whichever is lower.
distractors.why_benign: A capped late-payment charge on undisputed overdue amounts is aggressive but does not conflict with this playbook.

missing_info.topic: Processing geography and cross-border transfer basis
missing_info.context_anchor: The production service will process customer personal data across hosted systems.
Procurement requires certified deletion evidence when customer data exits the service.

generation_log: Base MSA drafted as original text for a customer-side vendor data-integration review.
D-001 applied by adding a later general-terms sentence that places Section 13 and Section 14 claims inside the Section 16 monetary limit.
D-002 applied by adding a transition-assistance payment sentence that neutralizes the earlier customer exit right.
D-003 applied by shifting the Deliverables definition to exclude Vendor Methods created during performance.
D-004 applied by narrowing the Security Incident definition to confirmed outbound extraction evidenced by a completed forensic report.
D-005 applied by removing downstream written obligations from the service-partner clause.
D-006 applied by replacing the 72-hour reporting period with thirty business days in the event notice section.
D-007 applied by replacing customer-approved one-year continuation with three-year additional periods unless notice is sent 180 days before period end.
X-001, X-002, and X-003 registered as aggressive-but-compliant distractors.
M-001 and M-002 registered as intentional missing information based on the client context.
